sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 4.76 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.4)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a locally finite group containing an element$a$of prime order such that the centralizer$C_G(a)$is finite. Is$G$almost soluble? { V.\,P.\,Shunkov} Yes, it is. P.\,Fong (Osaka J. Math., { 13} (1976), 483--489) has shown (mod CFSG) that$G$is almost locally soluble. Given this, B.\,Hartley and T.\,Meixner (Arch. Math., { 36} (1981), 211--213) have proved that$G$is almost locally nilpotent. Th...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
